peaceCorps.heading: What Is Peace Corps Colombia
peaceCorps.disclaimer: This is a personal website. The views expressed here are my own and do not necessarily reflect the views of the United States Government or the Peace Corps.
peaceCorps.intro: Peace Corps Colombia is an independent agency of the United States Government that works in partnership with Colombian communities and institutions to strengthen local capacities and promote sustainable development through the exchange of knowledge.
peaceCorps.whatItIsText: Peace Corps Colombia currently runs two main projects:
peaceCorps.howItWorksText: Volunteers live in and integrate into their communities for approximately two years, working alongside local organizations, educational institutions, and municipal governments to support community-led initiatives.
peaceCorps.goalsTitle: The Three Goals of Peace Corps

goals.title: Goal 1
goals.text: Help the people of interested countries meet their need for trained workers.

goals.title: Goal 2
goals.text: Help promote a better understanding of Americans among the people served.

goals.title: Goal 3
goals.text: Help promote a better understanding of other peoples among Americans.

peaceCorps.exchangeText: Building genuine, trusting relationships is as important as the technical work of any project. Cultural exchange flows in both directions: volunteers learn as much as they contribute.
peaceCorps.practiceTitle: What Peace Corps Service Looks Like in Practice

practiceSteps.title: Initial Training
practiceSteps.text: Volunteers receive intensive training in language, culture, safety, and technical skills before beginning service.

practiceSteps.title: Community Integration
practiceSteps.text: Volunteers move to their host community and spend time building relationships and understanding local context.

practiceSteps.title: Collaborative Assessment
practiceSteps.text: Together with local partners, volunteers identify community needs and opportunities.

practiceSteps.title: Project Work
practiceSteps.text: Volunteers co-facilitate workshops, support local initiatives, and contribute to jointly defined projects.

practiceSteps.title: Reflection and Close of Service
practiceSteps.text: Volunteers document lessons learned, help ensure project sustainability, and make a responsible transition at the end of service.

cedRole.heading: My Community Economic Development (CED) Role
cedRole.intro: The Community Economic Development (CED) Project works to strengthen the capacities of people, organizations, and institutions to contribute to local, sustainable economic development in Colombia.
objectives: Promote the personal, economic, and business development of youth, entrepreneurs, and community organizations.
Strengthen capacities for creating, managing, and growing small businesses.
Foster financial literacy and the responsible use of economic resources.
cedRole.whoWeWorkWithClosing: The goal isn't to create dependency, but to build capacities that let communities keep growing on their own.
cedRole.responsibilitiesTitle: My Role as a CED Volunteer
cedRole.responsibilitiesText: As a Peace Corps Community Economic Development Volunteer, I collaborate with educational institutions, community organizations, and local entities to strengthen entrepreneurship, employability, and economic-development skills. My work involves accompanying local partners in developing sustainable projects, sharing knowledge, and working collaboratively with the community.
cedRole.collaborativeNatureTitle: The Collaborative Nature of the Role
cedRole.collaborativeNatureText: All CED work is carried out in collaboration with local partners. Projects are co-designed and co-facilitated; outcomes reflect a shared effort between volunteers and communities.
cedRole.institutionsIntro: In the classroom, I collaborate with teachers through a co-teaching model to design and implement activities aimed at developing entrepreneurial and socioemotional skills in students. Initiatives developed include:
institutionsList: Entrepreneurship and financial-literacy curricula (such as Construye Tus Sueños)
Educational resources related to entrepreneurship and economic development
cedRole.communityWorkIntro: Beyond work in educational institutions, I collaborate with local organizations and partners to strengthen community capacities through initiatives such as:
communityWorkList: Support for entrepreneurs and small-business owners
cedRole.communityWorkClosing: These activities are carried out together with partners such as SENA, universities, municipal governments, community organizations, and other local institutions, responding to the needs and priorities of each community.

projects.heading: Projects
projects.intro: Each project on this site reflects a need identified by the community and a solution developed together with local stakeholders.
